raw_consumer_statement:
I made a purchase with XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX for {$100.00}. I tried to make a return with them, but 2 weeks after they received the item back I still had not received my return. 

I didn't get a response when I inquired by email, so I started a dispute with Citi bank for {$95.00} which would have been my refund amount. Citi bank gave me a conditional credit for that amount. 

After I already started the dispute, XXXX XXXX XXXX finally approved my refund and it showed as pending on my account, then disappeared and never posted as I had gotten the conditional credit from Citi bank. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I received notice that the dispute was resolved in the merchant 's favor and the conditional credit was reversed. 
I reopened the dispute and showed proof that the item had already been returned. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, I received notification that the dispute was resolved in my favor and the conditional credit would be permanent. 
But my transaction history still shows the reversal of the credit, so I'm still out the original amount I paid. 
Citi is denying this. They are referring to the credit showing on my statements but those are not accurate since the refund from XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX was reversed and the conditional credit was reversed. 
When I look at my transactions, it shows the original purchase for {$100.00}, the conditional credit for {$95.00} and the conditional credit reversal for {$95.00}, so I'm still out the {$100.00} I originally paid minus {$10.00} return fee. 

I would like Citi to refund me {$95.00}.
